enter.speaker: narrator
enter.text: A figure sits in the corner near the spare bed, knees drawn up, a wool blanket pulled around her shoulders. She has the gauntness of someone who has not slept properly in a week. The guild crest on her sleeve has been picked at with a knife — the threads still hang.

hesta-look.speaker: hesta
hesta-look.text: ...You came from the hall. I knew someone would. I told myself it would not be soon, and that was not true, and now it is. Sit down. Or do not. I do not know how to do this part.

hesta-question.speaker: hesta
hesta-question.text: Brenna sent you. So she still thinks of me as missing. That is a kindness she does not owe me.

hesta-patrol.speaker: hesta
hesta-patrol.text: Three of us went in. Wolves on the second night. Not the ordinary kind — bigger, quieter, the eyes wrong. My partner went down before he saw them. I held the ridge until I did not. I ran. The other one — Marek — I do not know. I did not look back long enough to know.

hesta-why-soft.speaker: hesta
hesta-why-soft.text: I sat in the bramble until morning. When I stopped shaking, I was not the woman who had walked into those woods. The badge on my sleeve felt heavier than the blade. I cut the threads. I came here because Orric does not ask. I have been sweeping his porch for my keep.

hesta-why.speaker: hesta
hesta-why.text: Because the next time I would have run anyway, and I knew it. The guild does not need a swordhand who knows she will run. The guild has enough of those buried already.

hesta-plea.speaker: hesta
hesta-plea.text: I am asking you. Not telling. Tell Brenna I died in the wolves. Tell her my partner held me and told me to go. Tell her anything but the truth, because the truth puts a notice on the wall and a price on a back I cannot turn fast enough anymore.

hesta-return.speaker: hesta
hesta-return.text: I cannot. I will not lift a sword again, and a guild without a sword is a coward in cloth. Walk out the door and choose what to say. I will be here, or I will be gone. Either way it is no longer your problem after the saying.

hesta-met-flag.speaker: narrator
hesta-met-flag.text: She turns her face to the wall. The blanket settles around her like the shoulders of something already buried. The cabin is very quiet.
